dependencies.relationship: uses APIGroup, LoadConfig, APIError, CacheMiddleware, CachePublic, FormatUTC, NowUTC from server_core
uses db.WithTx, db.WrapError, db.FormatTime, db.DB from database_layer

task_groups.title: Implement Provider Interface, UserInfo Struct, and Config Extensions

subtasks.title: Define Provider interface and UserInfo struct in internal/oauth/registry.go
details: Create internal/oauth/registry.go with package declaration `package oauth`

Define Provider interface with methods: Name() string, AuthorizeURL(state, redirectURI string) string, Exchange(ctx context.Context, code, redirectURI string) (string, error), UserInfo(ctx context.Context, accessToken string) (*UserInfo, error)
Define UserInfo struct: Username string, Email string, ProviderID string
Import context package

subtasks.title: Implement Registry type with NewRegistry, Register, Get, and List
details: Add Registry struct with providers map[string]Provider to registry.go
Implement NewRegistry() *Registry returning &Registry{providers: make(map[string]Provider)}
Implement Register(p Provider) error: return fmt.Errorf if p.Name() already exists; otherwise store in map
Implement Get(name string) Provider: return providers[name] (nil if missing)
Implement List() []string: collect keys, sort with sort.Strings, return
Import fmt and sort packages

subtasks.title: Extend Config struct with OAuthConfig and ProviderConfig types
details: Add OAuthConfig struct: Providers []ProviderConfig `toml:"providers"`
Add ProviderConfig struct: Name, ClientID, ClientSecret, AuthorizeURL, TokenURL, UserinfoURL string with TOML tags
Add OAuth OAuthConfig `toml:"oauth"` field to existing Config struct in internal/config package
In LoadConfig(), after unmarshalling: for each ProviderConfig check name/client_id/client_secret non-empty; check for duplicate names using a seen map; return (nil, error) on violations
Empty Providers slice is valid — skip validation loop

subtasks.title: Re-export Provider and UserInfo as type aliases in root apikit package
details: In root apikit package (apikit.go or oauth.go): add `type Provider = oauth.Provider` and `type UserInfo = oauth.UserInfo`
Import internal/oauth package
Ensure consuming projects can implement apikit.Provider without importing internal/oauth directly

checks: Spec tests for interface, registry, and config pass: go test ./internal/oauth/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestProvider|TestRegistry|TestConfig'
All existing tests still pass: go test ./... -count=1
No linter warnings: go vet ./...
Requirements 06-REQ-1.1, 06-REQ-1.2, 06-REQ-1.3, 06-REQ-2.1 through 06-REQ-2.4, 06-REQ-4.1, 06-REQ-4.2, 06-REQ-4.3, 06-REQ-4.5 acceptance criteria met

task_groups.title: Implement GitHub Provider and Redirect URI Validator

subtasks.title: Implement GitHub provider constructor and AuthorizeURL in internal/oauth/github.go
details: Create internal/oauth/github.go with GitHubProvider struct: clientID, clientSecret, authorizeURL, tokenURL, userinfoURL string, httpClient *http.Client
Define constants for default URLs: defaultAuthorizeURL, defaultTokenURL, defaultUserinfoURL
Implement NewGitHubProvider(clientID, clientSecret, authorizeURL, tokenURL, userinfoURL string, httpClient *http.Client) *GitHubProvider; use defaults when override fields are empty
Implement Name() string returning "github"
Implement AuthorizeURL(state, redirectURI string) string: build URL with url.Values setting client_id, scope=user:email, state, redirect_uri; append to base authorize URL

subtasks.title: Implement GitHub Exchange method
details: Implement Exchange(ctx context.Context, code, redirectURI string) (string, error)
Use http.NewRequestWithContext with POST method and form-encoded body (url.Values{client_id, client_secret, code, redirect_uri})
Set Accept: application/json header
Use g.httpClient.Do(req) for the call; check for non-2xx status and return error
Decode JSON response into struct with Error string and AccessToken string fields
Return error if response.Error is non-empty; return error if AccessToken is empty; otherwise return (accessToken, nil)

subtasks.title: Implement GitHub UserInfo method
details: Implement UserInfo(ctx context.Context, accessToken string) (*UserInfo, error)
Use http.NewRequestWithContext with GET method to userinfoURL
Set Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> header
Use g.httpClient.Do(req); check non-2xx status
Decode JSON into struct with Login string, Email string, ID int64 (json:"id")
Map: Login→UserInfo.Username, Email→UserInfo.Email, strconv.FormatInt(ID, 10)→UserInfo.ProviderID
Return (*UserInfo, nil) on success

subtasks.title: Implement redirect URI validator in internal/oauth/redirect.go
details: Create internal/oauth/redirect.go
Implement ValidateRedirectURI(redirectURI, externalURL string) error
Parse redirectURI with url.Parse; return error on parse failure
Check localhost: scheme == 'http' AND host matches regexp `^localhost:\d{1,5}$` with port in range 1-65535 → return nil
If externalURL non-empty: parse it; check redirectURI scheme+host equals externalURL scheme+host → return nil
Otherwise return fmt.Errorf('redirect_uri is not allowed')

subtasks.title: Implement server bootstrap: BuildRegistryFromConfig and shared HTTP client
details: Implement BuildRegistryFromConfig(cfg Config, httpClient *http.Client) (*Registry, error)
Create NewRegistry(); iterate cfg.OAuth.Providers
For name=="github": call NewGitHubProvider with fields and httpClient; call registry.Register(provider)
For unknown names: return (nil, fmt.Errorf("unsupported oauth provider: %s", name))
In server bootstrap entry point: create *http.Client{Timeout: 30*time.Second, Transport: http.DefaultTransport} and pass to BuildRegistryFromConfig

checks: GitHub provider unit tests pass: go test ./internal/oauth/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestGitHub'
Redirect URI validator tests pass: go test ./internal/oauth/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestRedirect|TestValidate'
Registry bootstrap tests pass: go test ./internal/oauth/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestBuildRegistry'
All existing tests still pass: go test ./... -count=1
No linter warnings: go vet ./...
Requirements 06-REQ-3.1 through 06-REQ-3.5, 06-REQ-7.1 through 06-REQ-7.3, 06-REQ-4.4, 06-REQ-13.2 acceptance criteria met

task_groups.title: Implement GET /auth/providers Handler and API Key Generation

subtasks.title: Implement GET /auth/providers handler in internal/oauth/providers.go
details: Create internal/oauth/providers.go
Define providerResponse struct: Name string `json:"name"`, AuthorizeURL string `json:"authorize_url"`
Implement handleListProviders(registry *Registry) echo.HandlerFunc
Call registry.List() to get sorted names; for each name call registry.Get(name).AuthorizeURL("", "") to get base URL without state or redirect_uri
Build []providerResponse slice; return c.JSON(http.StatusOK, slice) — Echo serializes empty slice as [] not null
Secrets (client_secret, token_url, userinfo_url) must never appear in the response struct

subtasks.title: Implement API key generation helper in internal/oauth/callback.go
details: Create internal/oauth/callback.go
Implement generateAlphanumeric(n int, randReader io.Reader) (string, error) using crypto/rand via randReader; characters from [a-zA-Z0-9]
Implement generateAPIKey(randReader io.Reader) (keyID, secret string, err error): call generateAlphanumeric(8) for keyID, generateAlphanumeric(32) for secret
Compute SHA-256 hash: sha256.Sum256([]byte(secret)); format as hex string for storage
Compute expires_at: nil when expires==0; NowUTC().Add(time.Duration(expires)*24*time.Hour) otherwise
Format key as apikit.TokenPrefix+"_"+keyID+"_"+secret
Do NOT import math/rand; only use crypto/rand

subtasks.title: Implement RegisterOAuthHandlers function
details: Implement RegisterOAuthHandlers(group *echo.Group, registry *Registry, database *db.DB, externalURL string)
Register GET /auth/providers: group.GET("/auth/providers", handleListProviders(registry), CacheMiddleware(CachePublic))
Register POST /auth/callback: group.POST("/auth/callback", handleCallback(registry, database, externalURL))
POST /auth/callback inherits group-level Cache-Control: no-store middleware
Re-export RegisterOAuthHandlers in root apikit package if needed for bootstrap access

checks: GET /auth/providers integration tests pass: go test ./internal/oauth/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestListProviders|TestProviders'
Key generation tests pass: go test ./internal/oauth/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestKey|TestGenerate'
Handler registration tests pass: go test ./internal/oauth/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestRegisterHandlers'
All existing tests still pass: go test ./... -count=1
No linter warnings: go vet ./...
Requirements 06-REQ-5.1 through 06-REQ-5.4, 06-REQ-11.2 through 06-REQ-11.4, 06-REQ-13.1 acceptance criteria met

subtasks.title: Implement request parsing and validation in handleCallback
details: Define callbackRequest struct: Provider, Code, RedirectURI string; Expires *int (pointer to detect absence)
In handleCallback: bind JSON body using c.Bind(&req); return APIError(400) on bind failure
Validate non-empty Provider, Code, RedirectURI — return APIError(400) with specific messages per field
Default Expires to 90 when nil; validate Expires is one of {0, 30, 60, 90} — return APIError(400) with 'expires must be 0, 30, 60, or 90'
Look up provider: registry.Get(req.Provider); if nil return APIError(400, fmt.Sprintf('unknown provider: %s', req.Provider))

subtasks.title: Implement redirect URI validation, code exchange, and user info retrieval in handleCallback
details: Call ValidateRedirectURI(req.RedirectURI, externalURL); return APIError(400, 'redirect_uri is not allowed') on error
Call provider.Exchange(c.Request().Context(), req.Code, req.RedirectURI); return APIError(401, 'authorization code exchange failed') on error
Call provider.UserInfo(c.Request().Context(), accessToken); return APIError(502, 'failed to retrieve user info from provider') on error
Check userInfo.Email non-empty; return APIError(400, 'provider returned empty email; email is required') if empty

subtasks.title: Implement user upsert within db.WithTx transaction
details: Open transaction with db.WithTx; query users WHERE provider=req.Provider AND provider_id=userInfo.ProviderID
If existing user: check status=='blocked' → return error causing rollback, handler returns APIError(403, 'user is blocked')
If existing user and not blocked: UPDATE users SET username=?, email=?, updated_at=? WHERE id=?
If new user: uuid.New() for ID; check admin_config for admin_email; if email matches AND no admin exists → role='admin' else role='user'; INSERT users with status='active', timestamps from NowUTC()
Handle missing admin_email (empty table) gracefully: treat as no admin_email configured; default to role='user'
Use db.WrapError on database errors; return HTTP 500 via APIError on wrapped errors

subtasks.title: Implement key revocation and new key insertion within same transaction
details: Within db.WithTx: UPDATE api_keys SET revoked_at=? WHERE user_id=? AND revoked_at IS NULL (no-op if no active keys)
Call generateAPIKey(crypto/rand.Reader) → (keyID, secret, err); return error on failure (causes rollback → HTTP 500)
Compute secretHash = hex(sha256.Sum256([]byte(secret)))
Compute expiresAt: nil if expires==0; NowUTC().Add(duration) otherwise
INSERT api_keys (id=uuid.New(), user_id, key_id, secret_hash, expires_at, created_at)
Commit transaction (db.WithTx commits on nil return from callback)

subtasks.title: Implement callback success response serialization
details: Define callbackResponse struct with nested User and APIKey fields
User fields: ID, Username, Email, FullName, Status, Role, Provider, ProviderID, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt (all RFC 3339 via FormatUTC())
APIKey fields: Key (TokenPrefix+_+keyID+_+secret), KeyID, ExpiresAt (*string, nil when expires==0)
Use FormatUTC() for all timestamps; null JSON for nil ExpiresAt
Return c.JSON(http.StatusOK, callbackResponse{...}) — plaintext secret appears exactly once here

task_groups.title: Wiring Verification: End-to-End OAuth Flow Integration

subtasks.title: Trace execution paths and verify no stubs remain
details: Run TS-06-SMOKE-5: server startup with config registers github provider in registry; GET /auth/providers returns 200; POST /auth/callback returns 400 not 404
Trace 06-PATH-1: verify CLI→handler→registry.Get→ValidateRedirectURI→Exchange→UserInfo→db.WithTx→response chain has no nil returns or skipped steps
Trace 06-PATH-2: verify returning user upsert path updates existing row and revokes old key
Trace 06-PATH-3: verify GET /auth/providers→registry.List→AuthorizeURL→JSON response chain
Trace 06-PATH-4: verify blocked user detection within db.WithTx rolls back and returns HTTP 403
Trace 06-PATH-5: verify LoadConfig→BuildRegistryFromConfig→RegisterOAuthHandlers startup chain
Search for panic("not implemented"), bare `return nil` in non-trivial paths, and `// TODO` markers: grep -r 'panic("not implemented")\|// TODO' internal/oauth/ — must return no results

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation across all handler steps
details: Confirm accessToken from Exchange is passed to UserInfo (not discarded)
Confirm userInfo.ProviderID is used in both the DB query and the response
Confirm keyID and plaintext secret from generateAPIKey are used: keyID stored in api_keys AND returned in response; secret only returned in response, never stored
Confirm expiresAt calculation is used in both api_keys INSERT and response serialization
Confirm db.WrapError is called on all database errors before returning HTTP 500

subtasks.title: Verify upstream spec entry points are called from production code
details: Confirm APIGroup() from spec 01 is called in production bootstrap before RegisterOAuthHandlers
Confirm CacheMiddleware(CachePublic) from spec 01 is applied to GET /auth/providers route (not just in tests)
Confirm db.WithTx from spec 02 is called in the callback handler (not mocked in production code path)
Confirm db.WrapError from spec 02 is imported and used in error mapping
Confirm FormatUTC() and NowUTC() from spec 01 are used for all timestamp handling in response serialization
Run: grep -r 'APIGroup\|CacheMiddleware\|db.WithTx\|db.WrapError\|FormatUTC\|NowUTC' internal/oauth/ — all must appear

subtasks.title: Run all smoke tests with real components
details: Run all five smoke tests with real SQLite DB, real Echo server, real oauth registry, and httptest.Server for GitHub endpoints
TS-06-SMOKE-1: new user created; api_key.key matches ^ak_[a-zA-Z0-9]{8}_[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}$; secret_hash==SHA256(plaintext)
TS-06-SMOKE-2: existing user updated; previous key revoked; new key issued
TS-06-SMOKE-3: provider list returns no secrets; Cache-Control header correct
TS-06-SMOKE-4: blocked user returns HTTP 403; DB unchanged
TS-06-SMOKE-5: startup registers github; both endpoints reachable
Command: go test ./internal/oauth/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestSmoke'

subtasks.title: Final dead-code audit and full test suite run
details: Run full test suite: go test ./... -count=1 — all tests must pass
Run linter: go vet ./... — zero warnings
Dead-code audit: grep -r 'panic("not implemented")' internal/oauth/ — must return empty
Verify math/rand is not imported in any file under internal/oauth/: grep -r '"math/rand"' internal/oauth/ — must return empty
Verify crypto/rand is imported in key generation file: grep -r '"crypto/rand"' internal/oauth/ — must return at least one result
Verify all error handling entries 06-ERR-1 through 06-ERR-15 have corresponding handler branches in callback.go

checks: Full test suite passes: go test ./... -count=1
Zero linter warnings: go vet ./...
All five smoke tests pass with real components: go test ./internal/oauth/... -count=1 -v -run 'TestSmoke'
No panic("not implemented") markers in internal/oauth/: grep -r 'panic("not implemented")' internal/oauth/ returns empty
No math/rand import in internal/oauth/: grep -r '"math/rand"' internal/oauth/ returns empty
All execution paths 06-PATH-1 through 06-PATH-5 verified as fully wired with no stubs
All correctness properties 06-PROP-1 through 06-PROP-8 validated by passing tests
All error handling entries 06-ERR-1 through 06-ERR-15 have corresponding production code branches
